rulings.Case Title: DANISHIA DAVID VS. CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal As A Terminating Sanction; Attorneys' Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Lynn Hollenbeck In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, April 15, 2025, line 2, DEFENDANTS MALCOLM YEUNG AN INDIVIDUAL, CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER,INC'S Motion For Dismissal As A Terminating Sanction; Attorneys' Fees And Costs On its own motion, the court continues the matter for hearing to April 23, 2025 to join defendants' other discovery motion set for hearing that day. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: NKECHI EMERUWA-NEUBERG VS. FRANK LUCCHETTI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT'S DEPOSITION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, April 15, 2025, line 10, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT'S DEPOSITION Plaintiff Nkechi Emeruwa-Neuberg moves to compel defendant Frank Lucchetti's deposition and production of documents. Emeruwa-Neuberg also seeks sanctions. The motion is granted in part and denied in part. Lucchetti is ordered to sit for deposition in this matter within five court days of entry of this order if he has not already done so. The court denies the motion to compel production of documents because responsive documents were previously produced and because Emeruwa-Neuberg has not set forth specific facts showing good cause justifying the production. (CCP 2025.450(b)(1).) Emeruwa-Neuberg's reply briefing justifying her particular document requests comes too late. The court denies the request for sanctions. It appears both parties have conditioned their witnesses' availability for deposition on the other party's conduct. That is a misuse of the discovery process and the court finds both parties' positions in that regard to lack substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO JUNETEENTH, VS. AFRICAN AMERICAN ART AND CULTURE COMPLEX, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Dismiss Complaint For Failure To Amend And/Or Retain Counsel; Declaration Of Antoinette P. Hewitt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, April 15, 2025, line 3, DEFENDANTS MELONIE GREEN AN INDIVIDUAL, AFRICAN AMERICAN ART AND CULTURE COMPLEX, INC, MELORA GREEN'S Motion To Dismiss Complaint For Failure To Amend And/Or Retain Counsel The unopposed motion to dismiss this matter with prejudice is granted. On November 20, 2024, this court sustained defendants' demurrer to plaintiff's First Amended Complaint with leave to amend. The court's order did not set a deadline for amendment; therefore, the default rule of 10 days applied. (Rule of Court 3.1320(g) ["Following a ruling on a demurrer, unless otherwise ordered, leave to answer or amend within 10 days is deemed granted, except for actions in forcible entry, forcible detainer, or unlawful detainer in which case 5 calendar days is deemed granted."].) Plaintiff has not yet amended nor sought additional time to retain counsel to amend. Where a demurrer is sustained and the plaintiff fails to amend, dismissal with prejudice is the appropriate order. (See CCP 581(f)(2); Cano v. Glover (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 326, 330.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER XUE VS. HIGHFIELD HOSPITALITY INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, April 15, 2025, line 5, PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE'S MOTION TO STRIKE Amended ANSWER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Plaintiff Alexander Xue's motion to strike defendant Highfield Hospitality, Inc.'s First Amended Verified Answer is denied. Xue asserts uncertainty in which affirmative defenses apply to which cause of action. Because all of the causes of action rely on the same facts, the Amended Answer is most intelligibly read as applying all affirmative defenses to all causes of action. It is appropriate for the Amended Answer to plead by way of affirmative defense that Xue fails to state a claim. A request for attorneys' fees and costs of suit is not required to be asserted by counterclaim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: SPENCER JACKSON VS. GETAROUND, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF SPENCER JACKSON'S MOTION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, April 15, 2025, line 8, PLAINTIFF SPENCER JACKSON'S MOTION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT Attachment is purely a statutory remedy and a court may not order an attachment procedure that is not specifically provided for by the Attachment Law. (See Jordan-Lyon Productions, Ltd. v. Cineplex Odeon Corp. (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 1459, 1466.) Here, plaintiff Spencer Jackson seeks a right to attach order and a writ of attachment based on his showing of probable validity of a commercial claim based on a contract. Defendant Getaround, Inc. contests the amount he seeks to attach and contends there are procedural defects in his application that preclude relief. THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)
